records.ety: [L. extrinsecus; exter on the outside + secus otherwise, beside; akin to E. second: cf. F. extrinsque. See Exterior, Second.]

defs: Not contained in or belonging to a body; external; outward; unessential; -- opposed to intrinsic.
quotes: The extrinsic aids of education and of artificial culture.

defs: Attached partly to an organ or limb and partly to some other part; -- said of certain groups of muscles. Opposed to intrinsic.
